page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the custom Arm64-based Alibaba Yitian710 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.75 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data positions the Yitian710 processor in the average range for standard computational tasks. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 7235.59 and a single-core score of 1818.58. Memory performance is characterized by a high cached read bandwidth of 408.75 GB/sec, which drops to 74.83 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 27.01 GB/sec. While database operations and Redis SET performance remain average at 6.89 million operations per second, cryptographic performance is weak, as evidenced by a PassMark encryption score of 651.04, likely due to the absence of specialized hardware acceleration flags.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c8y.xlarge leverages the Arm64 architecture to deliver competitive resource density for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for containerized microservices, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and lightweight database operations. However, it is poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference or intensive cryptographic operations due to architectural limitations and lower vector instruction test scores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.xlarge is an Arm64-based virtual server powered by a 4-core Alibaba Yitian710 processor running at 2.75 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a compute-focused 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for general computing, memory bandwidth, and web serving, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 7235.59 and Redis throughput of 6.89 million operations per second. However, cryptographic and complex vector instruction performance is relatively weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for scale-out workloads, making it well-suited for static web serving, containerized microservices, lightweight database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.xlarge is an Arm64 server featuring a 4-core Yitian710 processor, 8 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for cost-efficient web serving and containerized workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.xlarge is an Arm64 virtual server with 4 dedicated Yitian710 cores, 8 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A dedicated Arm64 virtual server offering balanced compute performance and high memory bandwidth for containerized microservices and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a custom 4-core Alibaba Yitian710 Arm64 processor at 2.75 GHz.
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, offering a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio.
Delivers 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Achieves 6.89 million Redis SET operations per second and 6.85 GB/sec web serving throughput.
Best suited for static web serving, microservices, and multi-threaded decompression.
